calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Determining Ownership Of Bank Accounts
Off Calendar Jun-27-2018 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Order Determining Ownership Of Bank Accounts
Off Calendar May-30-2018 Continued To Jun-27-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Cigavic for and with Reverend Alan Matsui (Guardian Ad Litem for Hanaes Ono).
Laurie Gong for Barbara Horiuchi and Michael Lulu.

Mr. Cigavic stated that only one Nephew (Michael Jon Ogasawara) in Florida will be Ms. Ono's intestate Beneficiary, that the Guardian Ad Litem has responded in papers, that Ms. Ono is incapacitated and will pass away intestate, and that Michael has been given notice. Ms. Gong stated that Michael has received notice and has waived notice, that he has reviewed the documents online, that she inquired about contact from the Reverend and that Michael's position is contact is not necessary. Reverend Matsui stated that when he tried to communicate with Ms. Ono, she was unresponsive. Ms. Gong also stated that she believes waiver of notice is sufficient, that the Reverend has reviewed the assets and that she would like to have the hearing today. The Court continued the Petition for Order Determining Ownership of Bank Accounts to June 27, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the Guardian to have contact with Michael.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition For Order Determining Ownership Of Bank Accounts
Off Calendar May-14-2018 Continued To May-30-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Laurie Shigekuni for Barbara Horiuchi and Michael Lulu (Petitioners)
John Cigavic for and with Alan Matsui (Proposed Guardian Ad Litem)

Ms. Shigekuni stated that there are no objections regarding Guardian ad Litem, she has spoken with Michael John Ogasawara (Nephew/heir), and will send him notice. The 850 Petition for order determining ownership of bank accounts is continued to May 30, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court granted the appointment of Alan Matsui as Guardian ad Litem (subject to review of the order, received in open court).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition For Order Determining Ownership Of Bank Accounts
Off Calendar Apr-03-2018 Continued To May-14-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Laurie Gong and Martha Bridegam for Barbara Horiuchi and Michael Lulu.

The Court made reference to Probate Code 5301. Ms. Bridegam stated that the time period for analysis was in September of 2009 which was the time Chiyeko created the Trust. Ms. Gong and Ms. Bridegam spoke of Chiyeko's intent to transfer the bank account into the Trust, a glitch at the bank at the time Chiyeko attempted to transfer the account due to the bank requesting a notarized agreement, meetings that took place, hand written notes from Chiyeko that Hanaes is to receive her money, forensic accountings that were done, the tax returns reporting the accounts that match, Michael and the charitable beneficiaries having already received their shares, appointment of a Guardian Ad Litem for Hanaes, the bank account not being a joint account and all of the money belonging to Chiyeko, etc. The Petition for Order Determining Ownership of Bank Accounts is taken under submission for Counsel to submit said notes.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition For Order Determining Ownership Of Bank Accounts
Off Calendar Mar-05-2018 Continued To Mar-19-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Laurie Shigekuni and Martha Bridegam for petitioner Barbara Horniuchi.

Ms. Shigekuni stated that the petition deals with the intent of Chiyeko Ono as to several accounts. She said that she was the estate planning attorney for the decedent andset up her trust. She said she was told by Ms. Ono that there were several accounts set up in two names, and that the accounts with Ms. Ono's name appearing first were her individual property and funded solely be her, including account 5282. She said that Ms. Ono's retirement benefits payments, social security and annuity payments all went into account 5282. The added that she had filed earlier today a history of intent as to deposits made, and would be supplementing that to address pre-2008 deposits within the next few days. The matter was continued to March 19, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)
